Version
  • Getting Started
  • Getting Started

Getting Started

Installation

GreenhousesJL is not yet registered in the Julia General registry. Install it directly from the project directory:

using Pkg
Pkg.activate("path/to/GreenhousesJL")
Pkg.instantiate()

Dependencies

PackagePurpose
OrdinaryDiffEqTsit5Adaptive Runge-Kutta ODE solver
SciMLBaseODE problem interface
DelimitedFilesWeather file parsing

Weather data format

GreenhousesJL reads Typical Meteorological Year (TMY) files in the same tab-separated format used by the Greenhouses Modelica library:

#1
double tab(8280,10)   # time T_out RH_out P_out I_glob u_wind T_sky ...
0       4.9     95      100700  0       3       -5.03   16      800     0
3600    5.0     96      100700  0       3.5     -5.03   16      800     0
...

The columns used by the solver are:

ColumnVariableUnitDescription
1timesTime since start
2T_out°COutdoor dry-bulb temperature
5I_globW/m²Global horizontal irradiance
6u_windm/sWind speed at 10 m
7T_sky°CSky temperature (for IR radiation)

Values between hourly records are linearly interpolated at any solver time step.

Your first simulation

using GreenhousesJL
using OrdinaryDiffEqTsit5

# 1. Load weather
tmy = load_tmy("Greenhouses/Resources/Data/10Dec-22Nov.txt")

# 2. Create parameters, bare greenhouse (no heating)
p = GreenhouseParams(tmy; A=14000.0)

# 3. Initial conditions
u0 = initial_state(p; T0=278.15, T_soil0=276.15)
#  T0 = 5 °C for air/cover/floor
#  T_soil0 = 3 °C for all soil layers

# 4. Solve
prob = ODEProblem(greenhouse_rhs!, u0, (0.0, 604800.0), p)
sol = solve(prob, Tsit5(); reltol=1e-6, saveat=3600.0)

# 5. Extract results
times_h = sol.t ./ 3600          # hours
T_air   = sol[2, :] .- 273.15   # °C  (row 2 = air temperature)
T_cov   = sol[1, :] .- 273.15   # °C  (row 1 = cover)
T_flr   = sol[3, :] .- 273.15   # °C  (row 3 = floor)

State vector layout

The state vector u is organised as follows:

IndexVariableUnitPresent when
1T_covKAlways
2T_airKAlways
3T_flrKAlways
4T_wallKA_walls > 0
4 or 5T_soil[1] ... T_soil[N]KAlways (N = Nc−1 + Ns)
lastintegral_errorK·sKp > 0 (heating active)

Use state_size(p), has_walls(p), has_heating(p) to query the layout programmatically.

Exporting results to CSV

export_csv("my_simulation.csv", sol, p)

This produces a CSV with columns: time_h, T_air_C, T_cover_C, T_floor_C, T_out_C, T_sky_C, I_glob_Wm2, u_wind_ms, and (if heating is active) Q_heat_Wm2, Q_heat_W, E_heat_kWh.

Plotting results

GreenhousesJL includes built-in plotting functions. They require a Makie backend to be loaded before GreenhousesJL:

using GLMakie          # interactive windows (or CairoMakie for static PNG)
using GreenhousesJL

# ... run simulation ...

# Time-series: temperatures + weather + heating power
plot_results(sol, p)                            # interactive window
plot_results(sol, p; save_path="results.png")   # export to PNG

# 3D greenhouse geometry (interactive rotation with GLMakie)
view_greenhouse_3d(p)
view_greenhouse_3d(p; save_path="serre.png")    # export to PNG
Import order matters

Load your Makie backend (GLMakie or CairoMakie) before using GreenhousesJL. Otherwise the plot functions will raise an error.

plot_results output

Three panels:

  1. Temperatures: air (blue), cover (red), floor (brown), walls (orange), outdoor (grey), setpoint (green dashed)
  2. Weather: solar irradiance (orange) and wind speed (teal, right axis)
  3. Heating (if active): power in W/m² (red) and cumulative energy in kWh (purple, right axis)

view_greenhouse_3d output

An interactive 3D view showing:

  • Glass roof panels (transparent blue)
  • Wall surfaces (semi-transparent blue)
  • Floor slab (brown)
  • Ridge line (red) and gutters (grey) for asymmetric roofs

Convenience re-exports

GreenhousesJL re-exports ODEProblem, solve and Tsit5 from the SciML ecosystem, so you don't need to import them separately:

using GreenhousesJL    # provides ODEProblem, solve, Tsit5

prob = ODEProblem(greenhouse_rhs!, u0, (0.0, 604800.0), p)
sol = solve(prob, Tsit5(); reltol=1e-6, saveat=3600.0)
« HomeTutorial: Bare Greenhouse »
